reviews.review_msg: The 2 midterms and 1 essay was worth majority of the grade. The grading was extremely unclear because my classmates and I followed all the directions and instructions but ended up with a grade that didn't match our work. When I asked for clarifying questions on improving I only got vague answers that were already written in the feedback.

This class was terrible - be warned that the quizzes are worth A LOT. Each quiz is 10%*5=50% of ur grade. And they are VERY hard. Do urself a favor and prep like crazy with ur friends beforehand. Otherwise discussions and assignments are super easy. If your smart about the quizzes and do well, easy A+. Genuinely no idea why the quizzes r like this.

It was awesome to have professor Shield! The best part about this class was her availability, even responding to emails on weekends as needed. This course was actually very interesting, and I will admit, I was pleasantly surprised! Overall, if any other Beedie student wants to take their INDG requirement, this course with Alix is the way to go.

Gives very good feedback on assignments and is always willing to help if you email. Assignments were really easy (be specific in your responses) and lectures are recorded so you can watch whenever, very short lectures. Ensure to follow the guidelines given regarding the assignments, she always seems to find something to critique on no matter what.

Took INDG 210 and 201W online courses with Alix. No matter how much time I put in essay (1 days or 1 week), all grades are similar, 11-12 out of 15.

Received comments as "on the right track but", while the grades were between 60% and 70%. final grades are B- and B, but class average is B+

Should work for most of students in class, but not for me.

great prof! she handled the topics well and made sure we really understood them. super caring and knowledgeable. had 2 tests that were really easy if you studied (all material from the class with no surprises). attendance is mandatory as there are discussions worth participation points. as an Indigenous student myself I really enjoyed the class!
Great class, everything we learned was on the tests, so no surprises. Excellent slide decks, and I actually learned. Would recommend

Probably the best professor I've had thus far. Alix gives super in depth analyses of how you performed on assignments as well as how you can improve in the future, and she is the sweetest! Pretty easy class overall, and the way Alix structures her class is very likeable.

I had my first Indigenous Studies class with Dr. Shield. She's such a sweetheart and demonstrated her knowledge in the topics well. I was really interested in the course. We had to be tested by two quizzes, where are like exams. Review the slides enough to know what you write. Participation is group work and easy to earn.

meh being my first indg course. attendance is mandatory if you miss class you only got slides to read cuz lecs aren't recorded. didn't really like the class bc everything was in-person even the "quizzes" prof also didn't do much to prepare us aside from telling us to look over slides+emailed us guest speaker slides after the quiz so thats nice lol
